steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best states the main idea of the passage. The passage covers two things: Evelyn Cunningham's individual contributions to civil rights journalism, and the National Association of Black Journalists' broader effort to recognize and preserve Black journalism history.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Cunningham's achievements are often overlooked in countries other than the United States.: focuses too narrowly on Cunningham alone without capturing the institutional role of the NABJ. ✗
  • Choice B — The National Association of Black Journalists was founded in Washington, DC, in the 1900s.: addresses the NABJ but omits Cunningham's specific contributions that anchor the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — Black journalism in the United States has an important history that the National Association of Black Journalists helps to honor.: correctly captures both elements — the significance of individual journalists like Cunningham and the importance of professional associations in preserving the 'rich history' of Black journalism in the United States. ✓
  • Choice D — Black journalism has an interesting history both inside and outside the United States.: misrepresents the passage's scope or emphasis, focusing on a detail rather than the central argument. ✗
That is the correct answer.
